label: RAG Chat (documents Q&A)
summary: Use when a Next.js site needs a streaming chat assistant that answers from the site's OWN indexed content (RAG), not a generic chatbot (that is ai-chat). Ships a Vercel AI SDK chat route, a client chat component, a Drizzle/pgvector schema, retrieval + ingestion helpers, and a ready-to-run pgvector migration file. Requires a pgvector-capable Postgres and OpenAI; without real keys the route streams a canned demo reply (mock: canned) and the UI shows a discreet demo notice instead of crashing.

envVars.purpose: Server-side OpenAI key used for embeddings (text-embedding-3-small) and chat generation. Get it at platform.openai.com/api-keys. Tagged feature-runtime because isRagConfigured() gates every model call: missing or placeholder key → the route streams a canned demo reply (mock: canned) and the UI shows a discreet demo notice instead of throwing.

Postgres connection string for the document/chunk/embedding tables and pgvector retrieval; the database must have the pgvector extension and the shipped rag-migrations.sql applied. Tagged feature-runtime because isRagConfigured() rejects missing/preview/placeholder values and the chat degrades to the canned demo reply instead of a 500.

notes: Hard dossier: server routes + pgvector Postgres. Capability 'rag-chat' must NOT compete with 'ai-chat' (openai-chat owns generic chatbots) — explicit RAG intent only. Legacy import final wave: lazy OpenAI + lazy getDb()/getPool(), same UI-message stream contract as openai-chat, isRagConfigured() placeholder-guards on BOTH keys, mock: canned, pgvector migration shipped as rag-migrations.sql (no auto-migrate at boot). lastVerified carries the legacy source date until a preview/generate-test selects it (no-false-green).
